main_sections.title: Change of Heart
main_sections.prose_summary: Prabhat is a competitive boy who cannot bear losing. He participates only in games he is certain to win, and even then he plays with fierce seriousness. When a new student, Surya, joins his school, Prabhat is immediately drawn to challenge him at badminton — the sport he excels at. Surya turns out to be a gifted player who approaches the game with effortless joy: he laughs, cracks jokes, and remains relaxed throughout, unconcerned with the score. Realising he is about to lose, Prabhat secretly alters the scoreboard while Surya is distracted and claims victory. Despite winning, Prabhat cannot sleep that night. He is troubled not only by the guilt of having cheated but by the inexplicable sight of Surya's contentment in defeat. In the following days, Prabhat quietly observes Surya playing basketball — a sport at which Surya is frankly hopeless — yet Surya's delight in the game never dims. Through this sustained observation, Prabhat arrives at a quiet but transformative insight: a scoreboard is not necessary to enjoy a game. What matters is wholehearted participation and the pleasure of the moment. Gradually, Prabhat himself begins to change. He joins games like hide-and-seek with genuine enthusiasm, starts joking during badminton, and earns from his classmates the very compliment that once seemed beyond him — that he has great sporting spirit. The story traces a moral arc from compulsive winning to the discovery of joyful, honest engagement, with Surya serving as a wordless model of what sporting spirit truly means.

question_bank.stem: If Prabhat sensed that he would lose a game, he would not play it. He would take part only when he was sure of winning, even if the game lasted only for a minute. You could not stop him from playing the kind of games he was really good at, like badminton. (i) Complete the sentence suitably: Prabhat would play a game only if ____________________. (ii) Which game was Prabhat good at playing? (iii) Select the most suitable option to complete the sentence: This behaviour of Prabhat tells us that he was ________________. A. good at the game  B. sure to win  C. afraid of losing

Surprisingly, Prabhat could not sleep well that night... (i) Select the one option that tells us about the way Prabhat was feeling that night. A. hurt  B. unhappy  C. afraid  D. focused (ii) Why does Prabhat find Surya's behaviour 'surprising'? (iii) Fill in the blank: We can say that Surya's reaction to not winning was ____________________ (similar to/as serious as/different from) Prabhat's.
options: hurt
unhappy
afraid
focused

How did Surya's attitude differ from Prabhat's?
Do you think Prabhat's approach to games was healthy? Why or why not?
Why do you think sporting spirit is important?
Explain how the title of the story, 'Change of Heart', is suitable.

listening.transcript_text: Good morning, everyone. I know you all are excited about the Friday friendly matches. This Friday we shall have three matches — one for chess, the next for kho-kho, and finally one for badminton. I have put up the kho-kho teams' name list on the sports' notice board. Since this is a team game, your class VI-B shall be playing against the VI-D kho-kho team. The kho-kho match refreshments are being sponsored by Xydium Sports Club. Now the places for the matches — chess will take place in the 1st floor activity room with Vani as the scorekeeper. Kho-kho will be played in the sports ground where I myself will be the referee. For badminton, we have chosen the multipurpose hall on the ground floor. For the chess match, it has been decided that this match shall be played between Sarita and Nupur. Two matches will be played during the regular games period on Friday but kho-kho will be played in the 2nd and 3rd period. Remember the kho-kho team names are up on the Sports' Notice Board. Good luck!

tasks_verbatim.task_text: You will listen to an announcement. As you listen, circle the pair that was chosen to play chess. (refer to page 128 for transcript)

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Look at the situations given below. In pairs, take turns to use the three magic words — 'please', 'thank you' and 'sorry' — for the situations given below. Use the prompts given in the table to learn how to thank and apologise properly.

question_bank.stem: You are Vani — You want to request your friends to take you in their team during games period.
You are Somiwon — You want to tell Hitesh to help you with the cleaning of the classroom cupboard.
You are Sumit — You accidently spilled water on Nupur's desk.
You are Hitesh — You forgot to get Simran's Art file to school.
You are Kriti — You spoke rudely to Nupur.
You are Mohan — You wish to thank Hitesh for helping you practice for the Inter-House speech competition.
You are Meher — You are grateful that Simran helped you understand a chapter taught at school when you were absent.
You are Rima — You are thankful to Gita for helping you collect the notebooks from the staff-room.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Prabhat managed to change the scoreboard while Surya was looking elsewhere. Yet, he did not feel so happy that night. Unable to sleep, he decided to write down his feelings in his diary. Given below is the page where he wrote his feelings that night. Complete it with the help of the phrases given in the box below: [I won the match / I do not feel happy about it / I will lose the match / I managed to change the scoreboard / I felt very happy / Everyone congratulated me after my win / Surya did not feel bad / He had fun playing with me]

question_bank.stem: Today, I defeated Surya in a badminton match but 1. __________________________. He was playing very well and was getting the points easily. I was sure that 2. _____________________. So, when everyone was laughing at a joke he cracked, 3. _____________________________. No one noticed the change and 4. ________________________________. What surprised me the most was that he said 5. _________________ and we should play again. How could he be so relaxed after losing a match? He should have been sad if not crying over his loss! Though 6. _______________________________, I did not enjoy as I usually do. It really disturbed me that 7. ____________________ about losing the match. It is still bothering me. Is it because I changed the score? I do not understand myself.

main_sections.title: The Winner
main_sections.poem_text:
Evenings,
we play ball
next to the creek
in our neighbour's field.

We run so fast
I
can't
even
catch
my
breath.

When blue dark turns to black,
cold grass aches our feet,
trees creep close—
game's over.

Night wins!
main_sections.poem_appreciation_summary: This short poem by Georgia Heard captures the exhilaration and inevitable end of children's evening play. The central image is a group of children playing ball in a neighbour's field at dusk, running with such joyful abandon that the speaker cannot catch her breath. The typographic arrangement of the lines 'I / can't / even / catch / my / breath' visually mimics gasping, making the breathlessness physical on the page. The poem's tone is warmly nostalgic and full of kinetic energy. Its dominant literary device is personification: Night is cast as the ultimate winner who ends the game, the trees 'creep close' as darkness falls, and the grass 'aches' the children's feet with cold — inanimate elements taking on agency. The title 'The Winner' is a gentle irony — not a scoreboard or a player, but Night itself claims victory, reminding young readers that nature sets the boundary of play, and that the real pleasure lies in the playing, not in winning.

question_bank.stem: next to the creek — Clue: a small body of water. What does this tell us about where the children play?
I can't even catch my breath — Clue: the child has been running. What does this expression mean?
When blue dark turns to black — Clue: the colour of the sky changes from blue to black. What does it mean?

They continue to play till the evening when the blue sky turns ______________, and the trees are not clearly visible. They can feel their feet ______________, now that it is cold. Finally, they stop ______________ and go home as it is night.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us think and reflect — Read the given lines and answer the questions that follow.

question_bank.stem: We run so fast / I / can't / even / catch / my / breath. (i) Complete the sentence: The speaker needs to catch her breath because ____________________________________. A. everyone decides to walk  B. all the friends wish to go home  C. running fast results in being breathless  D. they need to do breathing exercises. (ii) Is the speaker alone? Why do you say so? (iii) What does the pattern in the lines tell us?
options: everyone decides to walk
all the friends wish to go home
running fast results in being breathless
they need to do breathing exercises

question_bank.stem: When blue dark turns to black, / cold grass aches our feet, / trees creep close— / game's over. / Night wins! (i) Give one reason why the grass is cold. (ii) Complete the sentence: The children's feet are aching because_________________. (iii) Why does 'Night' win? Night wins because the ___________________________. A. time for play has ended  B. children are tired  C. time to play at home begins  D. night follows day
options: time for play has ended
children are tired
time to play at home begins
night follows day

listening.transcript_text: Riddle 1: Run and pass, run and pass / Kick a black and white ball / Kick it straight into the goal / Watch for mud — don't fall. / What sport am I? [Football/Soccer] Riddle 2: Hold your breath, say Hu-tu-tu, / till you touch one or two. / Take care to keep a distance wide / As you run back to your team's side. / Which sport am I? [Kabaddi] Riddle 3: In the water, hold your breath, / The water is very cool, / Arms turn, legs kick / In the blue pool. / Which sport am I? [Swimming] Riddle 4: Breathe in, breathe out! / Practise every day just right, / You will be healthy and fit / Get energy and grow in might. / What am I? [Yoga/Exercise]

tasks_verbatim.task_text: There are many indigenous games in India. Kho-kho is an example of an indigenous game. Indigenous games are native to a region. Find out some native games of your state and other parts of India. Learn the rules of one such game and play it with your classmates under the supervision of your teacher.

question_bank.stem: Find out at least two indigenous games from your state. What are their rules? Share with the class.

main_sections.title: Yoga — A Way of Life
main_sections.prose_summary: This informational text introduces yoga as a physical, mental, and spiritual practice that originated in ancient India. The word 'yoga' derives from the Sanskrit root 'yuj', meaning 'to join' or 'to unite', symbolising harmony between mind and body, thought and action, and human beings and nature. The text traces yoga's systematisation to Sage Patanjali's Yoga Sutras, which described yoga as the means to quiet the mind. The passage then outlines yoga's well-documented health benefits under several categories: physical benefits such as improved flexibility, muscle strength, endurance, and reduced body pain; mental benefits such as stress reduction, improved sleep quality, and enhanced concentration through breathing techniques and meditation; and systemic benefits including immune system support and improved heart health. The text notes yoga's global recognition, citing the World Health Organisation's launch of the WHO mYoga app in June 2021 as a free tool to promote yoga practice worldwide. It closes with an important pedagogical point: yoga asanas are not merely physical stretching — the mind must be present with the body, each stretch coordinated with breath and awareness. A Sanskrit shloka from the Hatha Yoga Pradipika is included, emphasising the primacy of asana in hatha yoga for achieving steady posture, health, and lightness of body.

reading_for_comprehension.section_name: Let us read + Let us discuss + Let us think and reflect

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us do these activities before we read: 1. What do you know about yoga? Discuss in pairs and share with the whole class. 2. What is 21st June known for? What do we do on this day? 3. List as many words you know related to yoga. Share them with your classmates. 4. Let us play the game of Antakshari. The first word is 'yoga'. The next word must then begin with the letter 'a'. How many new words have you learnt?

question_bank.stem: Yoga is a practise that not only improves physical flexibility but also improves mental 1. ____________ and leads to a healthier 2. ______________. It involves a series of asanas, which increase muscular 3. ______________. Practising yoga also leads to a better body and mind 4. ______________ and reduces body pain such as the lower back. Moreover, the focus on breathing techniques and meditation makes the mind 5. ______________, lessens 6. ______________, and improves the quality of 7. ______________. This helps to adopt a more positive lifestyle.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us think and reflect — Answer the following questions.

question_bank.stem: What is the aim of yoga?
Explain the idea of unity in yoga.
What does the launch of the WHO mYoga app tell us about the importance of yoga?
Describe one long-term effect that the practice of yoga can have on a person's qualities.

listening.transcript_text: Namaste everyone! I wish to share with you all that I do simple yoga exercises with my grandfather. He has been practising yoga since he was 16 years old. My grandfather is fit and strong even at the age of 65. I am sure that this is the result of many good habits. I know that eating a balanced diet regularly and making yoga a part of his life have been hundred per cent effective. When I accompany my grandfather to the park, I see so many people — young and old — doing yogic breathing exercises. I like doing these exercises. Whenever I'm a little worried about an Inter-House competition, I always remember to do some of these exercises early morning that day. It relaxes me and makes me feel focused and calm. I plan on learning some asanas from my grandfather during the summer vacations. Wish me luck!

tasks_verbatim.task_text: You will listen to a girl talk about practising yoga. As you listen, select four true statements from the sentences given in the table.

question_bank.stem: Select the FOUR true statements from the following: 1. The girl sees people at the park doing breathing exercises. 2. Her grandfather began practising yoga sixteen years ago. 3. The girl practises some easy yoga exercises with her grandfather. 4. The girl practises breathing exercises to relax. 5. Her grandfather is fit even though he has untimely meals. 6. Her grandfather's regular yoga practise has kept him strong. 7. The girl wishes to learn asanas over the weekend.
options: The girl sees people at the park doing breathing exercises.
Her grandfather began practising yoga sixteen years ago.
The girl practises some easy yoga exercises with her grandfather.
The girl practises breathing exercises to relax.
Her grandfather is fit even though he has untimely meals.
Her grandfather's regular yoga practise has kept him strong.
The girl wishes to learn asanas over the weekend.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Participate in groups of four to share your 'yoga thoughts'. Take turns to speak and listen to others when they share their thoughts.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Do you know? A few seals of the Sindhu-Sarasvati Civilisation, dating back to 2700 BC, indicate that yoga was prevalent in ancient India... On 11th December 2014, the United Nations General Assembly (UNGA) with 193 members accepted the proposal to celebrate '21st June' as 'International Yoga Day'. Find out about two asanas and their benefits, and share your findings with your classmates and teacher.

question_bank.stem: Find out about two yoga asanas and their benefits. Share your findings with your classmates and teacher. You may visit: https://www.mygov.in/campaigns/international-yoga-day/ or https://www.un.org/en/observances/yoga-day
